warnings: ECDLP mode: abstract_measurement_depth_layers is a Toffoli-derived proxy for heuristics, not a compiled layer schedule from the undisclosed circuits.
code distance d from heuristic_union_bound (phenomenological model), not the paper-specific optimizer in Gidney & Ekerå 2021.
approximate_data_plane_physical_qubits = abstract_logical_qubits × physical_qubits_per_logical; excludes magic-state factories, routing, classical control footprint, and other non-data overhead.
layout_optimization scans patch scaling vs d; it is not a full device placement optimizer.

naive_serial_time_days = abstract_measurement_depth_layers × surface_code_cycle_time; assumes one code cycle per abstract layer, no parallelism, no reaction/distillation limits — not comparable to Table 2 wall-clock without the paper’s full schedule.

logical_fault_model.logical_cycle_time_microseconds is max(available components); not a compiled schedule. Add qec_decode_latency_microseconds / qec_measurement_round_time_microseconds on QEC YAML when pinning OSRE τ_cycle pieces.
logical_fault_model.logical_error_rate_per_cycle is a phenomenological proxy A·(p/p_th)^ceil((d+1)/2), not a paper-calibrated logical channel.
dashboard.magic_throughput: skipped; need positive ccz_factory_count (Table 2 scenario row) or schedule_model_v1.ccz_factory_count.
parameter_sensitivity: local finite differences are coarse proxies; use Monte Carlo for global uncertainty.

scenario.description: Canonical Oratomic gold path: Cain et al. (2026) neutral-atom modality (neutral_atom_cain_et_al_2026), quantum LDPC QEC profile (qldpc_cain_et_al_2026), Gidney & Ekerå CCZ magic (+ T-factory aux), and ECDLP secp256k1 logical envelope from Babbush et al. (2026) (`ecdlp_secp256k1_babbush_et_al_2026`). See `docs/validation_overview.md` (Cain / neutral atom) for literature context.

modality.primary_reference: Cain, M., Xu, Q., King, R., Picard, L. R. B., Levine, H., Endres, M., Preskill, J., Huang, H.-Y., & Bluvstein, D. Shor's algorithm is possible with as few as 10,000 reconfigurable atomic qubits (2026).
modality.notes: Abstract emphasizes neutral-atom demonstrations (fault-tolerant ops below threshold, hundreds of logical qubits, arrays of thousands of coherent atoms) and reconfigurable connectivity for LDPC-style stacks. Scalar gate/cycle/reaction proxies below are SQuaRE placeholders for report plumbing: replace with values pinned from the paper body (native error, syndrome extraction cadence, shuttling/reconfiguration) when extracting tables. The key ``surface_code_cycle_time`` is reused as an **effective QEC cycle / syndrome-round time** (microseconds) for naive serial-time heuristics even though the QEC profile is not a surface code.

qec.primary_reference: Cain, M., Xu, Q., King, R., Picard, L. R. B., Levine, H., Endres, M., Preskill, J., Huang, H.-Y., & Bluvstein, D. Shor's algorithm is possible with as few as 10,000 reconfigurable atomic qubits (2026).
qec.notes: Abstract cites “high-rate quantum error-correcting codes” as one lever alongside efficient logical instruction sets and circuit design. Distance heuristics below reuse the same phenomenological union-bound machinery as surface-code profiles (with explicit warnings in reports) until LDPC-specific distance optimizers are implemented. Patch formula uses a reduced constant vs Gidney & Ekerå surface patches to reflect “smaller physical overhead per logical” at a **fixed symbolic distance d** — refine against the paper’s pinned layouts.

magic.primary_reference: Gidney, C. & Ekerå, M. How to factor 2048 bit RSA integers in 8 hours using 20 million noisy qubits. Quantum 5, 433 (2021).
magic.notes: Table 2 lists three “ours” configurations differing by CCZ factory count and whether carry is distillation- or reaction-limited. Use the matching modality + layout scenario; counts are for factoring 2048-bit RSA under the paper’s full pipeline.

algorithm.primary_reference: Babbush, R., Zalcman, A., Gidney, C., Broughton, M., Khattar, T., Neven, H., Bergamaschi, T., Drake, J., & Boneh, D. Securing Elliptic Curve Cryptocurrencies against Quantum Vulnerabilities: Resource Estimates and Mitigations (2026).
algorithm.notes: Two compiled quantum circuits are claimed with complementary logical qubit vs Toffoli tradeoffs. Resource verification via zero-knowledge proof is described in the paper appendix; SQuaRE stores only published aggregate bounds. Add doi / arxiv to this header when formally published.

magic_aux.primary_reference: Gidney, C. & Ekerå, M. How to factor 2048 bit RSA integers in 8 hours using 20 million noisy qubits. Quantum 5, 433 (2021).
magic_aux.notes: Figure 1 (and its caption) states that past ~n ≈ 32786 the construction exceeds the CCZ factory error budget and the model switches to the T factory of Fowler et al. This file flags that branch; detailed T-factory YAML should cite the original factory reference when implementing throughput.

header.primary_reference: Cain, M., Xu, Q., King, R., Picard, L. R. B., Levine, H., Endres, M., Preskill, J., Huang, H.-Y., & Bluvstein, D. Shor's algorithm is possible with as few as 10,000 reconfigurable atomic qubits (2026).
header.notes: Abstract emphasizes neutral-atom demonstrations (fault-tolerant ops below threshold, hundreds of logical qubits, arrays of thousands of coherent atoms) and reconfigurable connectivity for LDPC-style stacks. Scalar gate/cycle/reaction proxies below are SQuaRE placeholders for report plumbing: replace with values pinned from the paper body (native error, syndrome extraction cadence, shuttling/reconfiguration) when extracting tables. The key ``surface_code_cycle_time`` is reused as an **effective QEC cycle / syndrome-round time** (microseconds) for naive serial-time heuristics even though the QEC profile is not a surface code.

characteristic_physical_gate_error_rate.source: Cain et al. (2026), abstract (fault-tolerant ops below threshold on neutral atoms; no single p_g in abstract)
characteristic_physical_gate_error_rate.notes: Order-of-magnitude exploratory proxy for phenomenological distance heuristics. The paper’s headline resource count depends on LDPC thresholds and native gates; refine from main-text error budgets.

surface_code_cycle_time.source: SQuaRE placeholder — effective QEC cycle / syndrome-round proxy for naive serial stacking
surface_code_cycle_time.notes: Not a surface-code cycle from this paper; rename in future schema if a neutral-atom-specific key is added. Tune using Cain et al. layer schedules once extracted.

classical_control_reaction_time.source: SQuaRE placeholder — reconfiguration / control latency scale for reaction-limited heuristics
classical_control_reaction_time.notes: Reconfigurable-atom architectures may spend time on moves and local control; refine from paper.

connectivity_model.source: Cain et al. (2026), abstract (reconfigurable atomic qubits; neutral-atom experiments at scale)

platform_class.source: Cain et al. (2026); paired QEC profile qldpc_cain_et_al_2026

coherence_time_t1_microseconds.source: Order-of-magnitude neutral-atom storage; abstract cites highly coherent arrays — refine from paper

coherence_time_t2_microseconds.source: Order-of-magnitude T2 proxy; refine from Cain et al. device model

single_qubit_gate_error_rate.source: Exploratory sub-threshold native gate proxy; abstract does not pin per-gate breakdown

two_qubit_gate_error_rate.source: Cain et al. (2026), headline stack uses characteristic_physical_gate_error_rate scale; this row mirrors two-qubit native proxy
two_qubit_gate_error_rate.notes: Keep consistent with modality characteristic_physical_gate_error_rate when updating that entry.

measurement_error_rate.source: Readout proxy for neutral-atom architectures; refine from paper

idle_error_rate_per_cycle.source: Idle / shuttling proxy; refine from reconfiguration model

correlated_noise_parameter.source: Baseline multiplier; OSRE correlated scaling not yet modeled in this profile

leakage_error_rate.source: Small leakage proxy for neutral-atom gates; refine from paper

fabrication_variability_proxy.source: Placeholder OSRE variability slot for neutral-atom stack

thermal_load_index_proxy.source: Placeholder OSRE thermal index; reconfiguration duty cycle not modeled here

control_plane_saturation_proxy.source: Placeholder OSRE control saturation; refine from AWG / routing model

header.primary_reference: Cain, M., Xu, Q., King, R., Picard, L. R. B., Levine, H., Endres, M., Preskill, J., Huang, H.-Y., & Bluvstein, D. Shor's algorithm is possible with as few as 10,000 reconfigurable atomic qubits (2026).
header.notes: Abstract cites “high-rate quantum error-correcting codes” as one lever alongside efficient logical instruction sets and circuit design. Distance heuristics below reuse the same phenomenological union-bound machinery as surface-code profiles (with explicit warnings in reports) until LDPC-specific distance optimizers are implemented. Patch formula uses a reduced constant vs Gidney & Ekerå surface patches to reflect “smaller physical overhead per logical” at a **fixed symbolic distance d** — refine against the paper’s pinned layouts.

code_family.source: Cain et al. (2026), abstract (high-rate quantum error-correcting codes)

logical_qubit_patch_physical_qubit_count.source: SQuaRE illustrative proxy (reduced constant vs surface 2*(d+1)^2); replace with paper-pinned LDPC footprint
logical_qubit_patch_physical_qubit_count.notes: Not a literal LDPC parity-check tensor layout. Keeps eval_numeric_formula_with_bindings(d) stable while signaling lower overhead than the surface patch formula in surface_gidney_ekera_2021.yaml.

logical_operations_implementation.source: Cain et al. (2026), narrative (efficient logical instruction sets + LDPC)

heuristic_surface_code_physical_threshol.source: Order-of-magnitude stand-in for LDPC threshold scale; replace with calibrated p_th for the cited code family.

heuristic_logical_error_prefactor.source: Leading coefficient in phenomenological logical error model (same functional form as surface heuristic).

heuristic_distance_min_d.source: Clamp for heuristic distance suggestions in reports.

heuristic_distance_max_d.source: Clamp for heuristic distance suggestions in reports.

qec_decode_latency_microseconds.source: SQuaRE LDPC decode-latency placeholder; refine from Cain et al.

qec_measurement_round_time_microseconds.source: SQuaRE measurement-round placeholder for LDPC stack

header.primary_reference: Gidney, C. & Ekerå, M. How to factor 2048 bit RSA integers in 8 hours using 20 million noisy qubits. Quantum 5, 433 (2021).
header.notes: Table 2 lists three “ours” configurations differing by CCZ factory count and whether carry is distillation- or reaction-limited. Use the matching modality + layout scenario; counts are for factoring 2048-bit RSA under the paper’s full pipeline.

primary_non_clifford_mechanism.source: Gidney & Ekerå, Quantum 2021 (Table 2; distillation strategy column)

ccz_factory_supply_abstract_measurement_.source: Calibrated so Table-2 RSA-2048 parallel scenarios (28 CCZ, ~0.31 d wall) clear demand vs N_ccz×R under the same depth and schedule_model_v1 wall-clock proxies as the report; replace with extracted factory throughput when available.

paper_table2_rsa2048_reference_rows.notes: Each row matches one “ours 2019” configuration. layout_descriptor steers simple schedule inference (distillation-limited vs reaction-limited). Reports match rows by ccz_factories from scenario table2_reference_row.

physical_qubits_per_ccz_factory_approxim.source: Order-of-magnitude placeholder for CCZ factory + local routing; replace with layout-extracted value when available.
physical_qubits_per_ccz_factory_approxim.notes: Report still prefers derived_non_data_overhead_physical_qubits (pinned total minus naive data plane) when paper_table2_rsa2048_reference_rows matches the scenario CCZ count.

header.primary_reference: Gidney, C. & Ekerå, M. How to factor 2048 bit RSA integers in 8 hours using 20 million noisy qubits. Quantum 5, 433 (2021).
header.notes: Figure 1 (and its caption) states that past ~n ≈ 32786 the construction exceeds the CCZ factory error budget and the model switches to the T factory of Fowler et al. This file flags that branch; detailed T-factory YAML should cite the original factory reference when implementing throughput.

t_factory_used_beyond_ccz_error_budget.notes: Discrete jump in resource estimates in Figure 1 attributed to switching from CCZ factory (Litinski 2019 / ref [36] in paper) to T factory (Fowler et al. 2012 / ref [27]).

modulus_bit_length_ccz_to_t_transition_o.notes: Stated as approximate location of the jump; not a sharp universal constant across all tunings.

fallback_non_clifford_mechanism.source: Gidney & Ekerå, Quantum 2021 (Figure 1 caption; cites Fowler et al. 2012)

header.primary_reference: Babbush, R., Zalcman, A., Gidney, C., Broughton, M., Khattar, T., Neven, H., Bergamaschi, T., Drake, J., & Boneh, D. Securing Elliptic Curve Cryptocurrencies against Quantum Vulnerabilities: Resource Estimates and Mitigations (2026).
header.notes: Two compiled quantum circuits are claimed with complementary logical qubit vs Toffoli tradeoffs. Resource verification via zero-knowledge proof is described in the paper appendix; SQuaRE stores only published aggregate bounds. Add doi / arxiv to this header when formally published.

cryptographic_problem.source: Babbush et al. (2026), abstract and introduction

ecdlp_logical_resource_envelopes_secp256.notes: Abstract states Shor’s algorithm for this problem can execute with either ≤1200 logical qubits and ≤90 million Toffoli gates or ≤1450 logical qubits and ≤70 million Toffoli gates. Downstream reports must map Toffoli budgets to logical depth / magic consumption using an explicit convention.

ecdlp_measurement_depth_layers_per_toffo.source: SQuaRE ECDLP report convention (documented default; tune with paper-specific scheduling when known)
ecdlp_measurement_depth_layers_per_toffo.notes: Phenomenological union-bound heuristic and naive_serial_time use this depth proxy; it is not a compiled circuit schedule. Set to 1.0 so each Toffoli contributes one layer to the depth product.

headline_superconducting_physical_qubits.source: Babbush et al. (2026), introduction (order-of-magnitude headline vs prior estimates)
headline_superconducting_physical_qubits.notes: Paper states that on a standard superconducting architecture with surface-code error correction, the computations could be realized with fewer than half a million physical qubits (context: comparison to prior estimates). Treat as narrative anchor, not a substitute for composed modality+QEC reports.

notes: Optional richness keys (fabrication_variability_proxy, thermal_load_index_proxy, control_plane_saturation_proxy) are surfaced for OSRE transparency when present in modality YAML; they do not enter heuristic p_effective, union-bound distance, or logical_fault_model in this version.

coherence_time_t2_microseconds.source: Order-of-magnitude T2 proxy; refine from Cain et al. device model

fabrication_variability_proxy.source: Placeholder OSRE variability slot for neutral-atom stack

two_qubit_gate_error_rate.source: Cain et al. (2026), headline stack uses characteristic_physical_gate_error_rate scale; this row mirrors two-qubit native proxy
two_qubit_gate_error_rate.notes: Keep consistent with modality characteristic_physical_gate_error_rate when updating that entry.

single_qubit_gate_error_rate.source: Exploratory sub-threshold native gate proxy; abstract does not pin per-gate breakdown

correlated_noise_parameter.source: Baseline multiplier; OSRE correlated scaling not yet modeled in this profile

measurement_error_rate.source: Readout proxy for neutral-atom architectures; refine from paper

leakage_error_rate.source: Small leakage proxy for neutral-atom gates; refine from paper

idle_error_rate_per_cycle.source: Idle / shuttling proxy; refine from reconfiguration model

control_plane_saturation_proxy.source: Placeholder OSRE control saturation; refine from AWG / routing model

thermal_load_index_proxy.source: Placeholder OSRE thermal index; reconfiguration duty cycle not modeled here

coherence_time_t1_microseconds.source: Order-of-magnitude neutral-atom storage; abstract cites highly coherent arrays — refine from paper

notes: LQC omitted: need reported_end_to_end_physical_qubits (e.g. Table 2 RSA pin), factory_footprint_physical_qubits_from_yaml, and evaluated physical_qubits_per_logical.

LOB uses ε / (p_L × s_QEM) with phenomenological p_L from logical_fault_model (p_L is driven by heuristic p_effective: p_nominal × QCVV σ × scaling penalty on that stack, not by validated_error_rate_ver, which is headline characteristic × σ only) and QEM effective_logical_error_rate_suppression_factor as s_QEM (1 when no QEM path).
QOT is abstract-depth-proxies per wall-clock second: parallel_width / τ with τ from logical_fault_model and width from schedule_model_v1.ccz_factory_count or scenario CCZ count.
VER omitted: no paths.qcvv (QCVV profile) in scenario.
mitigated_operations_ceiling omitted: no paths.qem (QEM profile) in scenario.
Finite differences use relative step 1e-5 on effective gate error and threshold; code_distance_d is discrete so derivatives are secants between perturbed optima.
